issues.title: feat(secret): introduce secret.Value opaque type and error-wrap machinery

summary.key_decisions: rework addressed reviewer blockers: strings.ReplaceAll instead of NIH helpers; direct type assertion in Wrap instead of errors.As (prevents silent drop of outer fmt.Errorf layers)

requirements_map.ac: secret runtime Issue 1 full AC list + reviewer blockers
requirements_map.evidence: see per-AC mapping in first commit; reviewer blockers fixed in 804b6d9

reviewer_results.resolution: Both blocking findings addressed in commit 804b6d9e6dc9fc83084bb2af7eaa9b85bd3b1d3b. Pragmatic F1 (hand-rolled replaceAll/indexOf) fixed by using strings.ReplaceAll. Maintainer B1 (Wrap errors.As drops outer layer) fixed by tightening to direct type assertion + regression test TestWrapDoesNotDropOuterLayer.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic F2: Error.Redactor accessor has no production caller
pragmatic F3: bytes() duplicates Bytes
pragmatic F4: nil-receiver guards on *Error
architect non-blocking: secret.Bytes export concern (mitigated by internal/ module boundary)
maintainer A1-A5: documentation polish items (see detail file)

issues.title: feat(vault): provider interface, registry, factory, and fake backend

summary.key_decisions: Issue 2 core + rework: fake backend not-registered-in-DefaultRegistry test now probes DefaultRegistry directly with rollback; added Registry.Unregister for test-only rollback

requirements_map.ac: Issue 2 full AC list + maintainer B1

reviewer_results.resolution: Maintainer B1 addressed in commit baf2e829b8c7342e9594f95e9fb0ae6355e5df9a: TestNotRegisteredInDefaultRegistry rewritten to probe vault.DefaultRegistry directly with rollback; added Registry.Unregister for test-only rollback; added TestRegistryUnregister covering round-trip, unknown-kind error, empty-kind error.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: fake.NewFactory() wraps empty struct; redundant strings.Contains("#") + parsed.Fragment check; Bundle.CloseAll sort comment overstates benefit; knownProviderNames single-caller helper
architect: Source attribution when ProviderSpec.Source is empty; Factory.Open once-per-spec doc clarification

maintainer advisory: Bundle.CloseAll could use errors.Join; closed-provider error vs network-outage; Provider.Resolve references secret.Errorf without package pointer; typo in Registry.Build doc; ProviderSpec.Source equality caveat placement

issues.title: feat(config): vault schema, env.vars/env.secrets split, MaybeSecret type

summary.key_decisions: Issue 3 core + rework: B1 reserved keyword detection moved to post-decode pass using toml.MetaData.Keys() to emit path-aware errors; B2 named/anonymous registry shape mismatch now produces actionable errors listing declared providers and both fix paths; shape struct computed once per Parse

requirements_map.ac: Issue 3 full AC list + reviewer blockers B1 B2
requirements_map.evidence: see coder returns with per-AC test mapping

reviewer_results.resolution: Both maintainer blockers addressed in commit 3d437f3: B1 reserved-keyword error now points to the sub-table user should use; B2 anonymous-vs-named URI mismatch error lists declared providers and both fix paths. Symmetric named-vs-anonymous error also added.
reviewer_results.out_of_scope_finding_for_issue_4: Architect flagged that internal/workspace/override.go doesn't merge override.Env.Secrets.Values — only Env.Vars is merged. This is a dormant merge gap surfaced by Issue 3's schema addition. Must be fixed in Issue 4 resolver/merge integration.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: VaultRegistry.IsEmpty() has no callers (speculative); redundant len check in coerceDescriptionMap; MaybeSecret.MarshalText unused
architect: MaybeSecret Plain XOR Secret not mechanically enforced (public fields); extractProviderName could route through vault.ParseRef
maintainer advisory: KnownProviderNames nil-safe call pattern; scaffold template visual separator; stale (Issue 4) pointer comments

issues.title: feat(workspace): resolver stage and apply.go pipeline wiring

summary.key_decisions: Placed resolver in internal/vault/resolve sub-package (not internal/vault/resolver.go) to avoid import cycle: internal/config imports internal/vault (for VersionToken in MaybeSecret.Token); a resolver in internal/vault operating on *config.WorkspaceConfig would cycle. Sub-package internal/vault/resolve depends on both while internal/vault stays config-free. Extended fake backend config to accept map[string]any for TOML-decoded values. Added Bundle.Names() for R12 collision check without exposing internal map. MergeGlobalOverride signature changed to (cfg, error) for team_only reporting; call site + fixtures migrated via mustMerge helper

requirements_map.ac: Issue 4 full AC list + Issue 3 architect finding (env.secrets merge fixed)
requirements_map.evidence: see coder return with 10 per-AC mappings

reviewer_results.resolution: No blockers — all three reviewers approved. 8 non-blocking advisories deferred (documentation polish, single-layer consolidation opportunities, naming nits).
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: walkFilesKeys no-op; SourceFile mostly test-only
architect: resolve.ResolveGlobalOverride vs workspace.ResolveGlobalOverride name collision; parallel deepcopy helpers; isVaultURI duplicates config.hasVaultPrefix
maintainer A1 (walkFilesKeys doc self-contradiction — worth fixing pre-merge but not blocking this issue); A2 (ResolveWorkspace ownership doc); A3 (stale effectiveCfg comment)

issues.title: feat(vault): Infisical backend with batch resolve and stderr scrub

summary.key_decisions: Issue 5 core + rework: VersionToken.Token derives from (key + value-length) not plaintext (architect blocker); auth-failure marker set tightened to explicit phrases (401/403/unauthorized/forbidden/not logged in/...); anonymous-provider error messages print '<anonymous>'; v1 coarse-grain trade-off documented — same-length rotations don't flip token, v1.1 will use infisical secrets get --format json for per-key version UUIDs

requirements_map.ac: Issue 5 full AC list + architect blocker + auth heuristic + cosmetic A3
requirements_map.evidence: per-AC mapping in initial coder return; rework addresses plaintext-derivation leak

reviewer_results.resolution: Architect blocker addressed in commit 39e5209: VersionToken.Token now derives from SHA-256(sorted key + big-endian value length) per key — plaintext never touched. v1.1 TODO to switch to per-key `infisical secrets get --format json`. Also tightened looksLikeAuthFailure (A1) and cosmetic A3 ('<anonymous>' for empty provider name).
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: NewFactory no production callers yet; subprocess nil-guard unreachable
architect A2 (ensureLoaded not single-flight — documented rough edge, sync.Once refactor deferred)
maintainer M1 (subprocess errors lack project/env/path context)
maintainer M2 (stale defaultCommander comment)

summary.key_decisions: Issue 6 core + rework: added Stderr io.Writer on FilesMaterializer with per-Materialize-call dedup map; EnsureInstanceGitignore now called from both Create and Apply (idempotent)

requirements_map.ac: Issue 6 full AC list + maintainer B1 + architect advisory

reviewer_results.resolution: Maintainer B1 addressed in commit 5b1d682 (FilesMaterializer emits stderr notice on .local injection, deduped per Materialize call). Architect non-blocking advisory also addressed in same commit (EnsureInstanceGitignore now called from Apply in addition to Create).
advisory_deferred: maintainer A1 (injectLocalInfix substring edge cases not tested)
maintainer A2 (materializeDir parallel-branch cleanup)

issues.title: feat(workspace): SourceFingerprint and per-source tuples in state.json

summary.key_decisions: ContentHash JSON tag kept as 'hash' to let v1 state files load via omitempty-based migration shim without custom Unmarshal path; SourceTuples added on MaterializeContext rather than changing Materializer.Materialize signature; ComputeStatus uses candidate-path walk to locate SourceID files without adding configDir argument; vault rotation visibility deferred to Issue 10's --check-vault flag

requirements_map.evidence: per-AC mapping in coder return (13 entries)

reviewer_results.resolution: No blockers — all three reviewers approved. 7 non-blocking advisories deferred (docstring drift, coverage gaps, display-label cleanup, brittle candidate-path walk).
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: recomputeChangedPlaintextSources comment mentions 3 candidates but code uses 2; missing-file branch coverage; synthesized-SourceID skip
architect: configDir not threaded into ComputeStatus; hook SourceID display shows raw absolute paths; looksLikePath colon heuristic
maintainer: CLI status.go uses raw 'vault'/'plaintext' literals instead of workspace.SourceKind* constants

issues.title: feat(workspace): shadow detection and diagnostics across apply/status

summary.key_decisions: Issue 8 core + rework: removed renderShadowedColumn and TestRenderShadowedColumn (Issue 10 scope leak); ownership note added to Shadow docstring

requirements_map.ac: Issue 8 full AC list + scope cleanup

reviewer_results.resolution: Pragmatic blocker addressed in commit 553ca1c: removed renderShadowedColumn and TestRenderShadowedColumn — Issue 10 owns --audit-secrets SHADOWED column. Ownership note added to Shadow struct docstring.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic non-blocking consumed by blocker fix
architect: attribution defaults live in two spots; renderShadowedColumn concatenates Layer verbatim (removed anyway); provider shadows not persisted (documented)
maintainer: apply.go hard-codes 'workspace.toml'/'niwa.toml' instead of using teamSourceDefault/personalSourceDefault constants from shadows.go

summary.key_decisions: Issue 9 core + rework: extracted isPlaintextSecret helper with 3-way classification (empty/unresolved-vault-URI/plaintext); skip unresolved vault:// URIs in guardrail so pre-resolve configs with legitimate vault refs aren't blocked; regression test covers pre-resolve shape

requirements_map.ac: Issue 9 full AC list + architect blocker

reviewer_results.resolution: Architect blocker (guardrail misclassified pre-resolve vault:// URIs as plaintext) addressed in commit a4c323f: extracted isPlaintextSecret helper with explicit 3-way classification; added regression tests for pre-resolve shape.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic A1 (remoteLineFields single-caller helper), A2 (overlap between regex-table and e2e tests)
architect A1 (case-variant URL dedup), A2 ('guardrail skipped' message collapses 3 branches)

issues.title: feat(cli): vault flags, status subcommands, bootstrap pointer

summary.key_decisions: Issue 10 core + rework: audit predicate now uses !cfg.Vault.IsEmpty() aligning with init.go and check-vault; IsEmpty() tightened so TeamOnly alone does not count as non-empty (doc already claimed this; code was inconsistent); printVaultRotations closes with 'Run niwa apply' call-to-action

requirements_map.ac: Issue 10 full AC list + maintainer blocker

reviewer_results.resolution: Maintainer blocker addressed in commit 6feef8e: audit predicate now uses !cfg.Vault.IsEmpty() matching sibling paths (init.go, check-vault); IsEmpty() tightened so TeamOnly alone is no longer non-empty (aligns with existing doc comment); printVaultRotations closes with call-to-action.
advisory_deferred: pragmatic: classResolved dead branch; pre-existing driftLabel dead branch in status.go:160-162; AllowFlags test binds cobra flags but doesn't verify runApply wiring
architect: showDetailView vs runStatus test parity (nice-to-have)
maintainer: printVaultRotations exit code not set when rotations detected; pre-existing driftLabel dead branch

issues.title: docs(vault): bootstrap walkthrough and acceptance coverage

summary.key_decisions: Issue 11 docs + rework: fixed B1 (niwa apply now emits 'rotated <path>' on vault-source changes), B2 (new workspace.checkRequiredKeys enforces *.required as hard errors + recommended warnings + optional silent per v1), B3 (vault_scope ambiguity check for multi-source workspaces with personal overlay); AC coverage matrix corrected for R34 row; TestArgvHygiene R28 gap documented in matrix (fixing out-of-scope)
